email.subject: Nassau University Medical Center — infant follow-up is working; maternal discharge prep lags
email.email_body:
Hi,

Newborns in New York show up for their well-baby visits at rates above 91%, yet new mothers often don't make it back for their own postpartum checkups. At Nassau University Medical Center, 69% of patients report receiving the discharge information they needed—and your care transition rating sits at 1 star. That gap between infant and maternal follow-up is real.

Remote postpartum monitoring closes it. Hospitals using Babyscripts saw patients become 2x more likely to complete their 30-day postpartum visit. The model is straightforward: a BP kit, mobile app, and OB-specialized care managers handle the logistics. You bill for RPM using standard CPT codes.

Given your Birthing-Friendly designation, this aligns directly with your commitment to maternal outcomes.

Are you seeing similar discharge information gaps in your postpartum population, or is follow-up completion already a strength?
